title: Caldera - LSASS Memory Dump via ProcDump or Task Manager (Caldera Emulation)
description: Detects LSASS credential dumping specifically as emulated by Caldera T1003.001 operations. Caldera's credential-access adversary profile leverages ProcDump, Task Manager, or comsvcs.dll MiniDump to extract LSASS memory. This rule covers the ProcDump command-line pattern and the Task Manager dump output file name, complementing the existing lsass_memory_dump_via_comsvcs_dll.yaml.
query: ('Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Events' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and ((('Process Name' like '*\\procdump.exe' or 'Process Name' like '*\\procdump64.exe') and ('Command Line' like '*lsass*' or 'Command Line' like '*-ma *')) or (('Command Line' like '*lsass.dmp*' or 'Command Line' like '*lsass.exe.dmp*')) or ('Process Name' like '*\\nanodump.exe') or (('Command Line' like '*avdump64.exe*' or 'Command Line' like '*avdump.exe*')))
falsepositives: Authorized memory forensics on production systems
Incident response tooling collecting crash dumps
